narrative:
On July 6, 2026, the Karnataka High Court managed a significantly expanded docket, scheduling 3,026 cases and hearing 1,268, resulting in an overall hearing rate of 41.9%. Today's total caseload represents a sharp volume increase compared to prior sessions, which ranged from 2,362 scheduled matters on June 30 to 2,430 on July 2. While the 41.9% hearing rate shows a decline from the 49.3% conversion rate recorded on July 2, it remains consistent with the court's earlier performance of 42.8% on July 1 and 41.2% on June 30.

Judicial performance varied widely under the increased workload. Justice M. Nagaprasanna in Hall 14 handled a combined docket of 464 matters, successfully hearing 222 cases for an active conversion rate of 47.8%. Similarly, Justice Suraj Govindaraj demonstrated strong clearance, hearing 172 of 271 scheduled cases (63.5%) across Halls 10 and 11. Conversely, high-volume benches like that of Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 scheduled 331 matters but converted just 27.5%, while the division benches in Halls 1 and 2 did not hear any of their combined scheduled matters.

From a stage perspective, the court prioritized "Orders," which constituted the largest share with 1,004 scheduled cases and a 42.3% hearing rate. "Admission" matters saw the highest clearance rate among major stages at 51.4% (162 of 315 heard), whereas "Final Hearing" matters lagged at a 24.0% conversion rate. In case-type distribution, Writ Petitions (WP) dominated the docket with 1,428 scheduled cases, achieving a 42.1% hearing rate. Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) followed with 254 of 567 cases heard (44.8%), while Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) posted the strongest performance among major case categories with a 52.6% clearance rate.
